sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.111 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Do there exist infinite groups all of whose proper subgroups are cyclic of order~$p$that do not satisfy any nontrivial group identity except$x^p = 1$and its consequences? Note that there exist infinite groups all of whose proper subgroups are infinite cyclic that do not satisfy any nontrivial group identity (due to A.\,Olshanskii; see P.\,Zusmanovich, J.~Algebra, { 388} (2013), 268--286, Remark af...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
